{"id":"project-LICHEN-eirg","title":"[cross-impl] hash_32 primitive inconsistent across Rust, C, Python vectors","description":"Review of last 20 commits (mostly bd metadata + test/vectors/generate.py conflict cleanup in f2a77d8aa) revealed persistent mismatch:
- hash_32.json uses CRC32 initialized with b"LICHEN" (zlib.crc32(b"LICHEN"+data)), expects 0x77f9adf0 for empty.
- Rust (lichen-core::lichen_hash_32, lichen-link::identity::hash_32 + tests) and python/tests/test_vectors.py::_hash_32 use FNV-1a32 (basis 0x811c9dc5, prime 0x01000193), expects 0x811c9dc5 for empty.
- C (lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:tuple_crc, lora_ping:packet_hash) uses crc32_ieee.
- Descriptions in README.md, schema.json, ccp*.json conflict on FNV vs keyed-CRC.
- ccp15_vectors() in generate.py (recently cleaned) uses unrelated 0x9e3779b9 multiplier for load_factor test.
Same named primitive produces different outputs across Python reference, Rust, Zephyr/C impls. Violates cross-impl consistency requirement for test vectors. See spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md and epic project-LICHEN-pgzj.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T01:59:01.853497662Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T01:59:01.853497662Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-eirg","title":"[cross-impl] hash_32 primitive inconsistent across Rust, C, Python vectors","description":"Review of last 20 commits (mostly bd metadata + test/vectors/generate.py conflict cleanup in f2a77d8aa) revealed persistent mismatch:
- hash_32.json uses CRC32 initialized with b"LICHEN" (zlib.crc32(b"LICHEN"+data)), expects 0x77f9adf0 for empty.
- Rust (lichen-core::lichen_hash_32, lichen-link::identity::hash_32 + tests) and python/tests/test_vectors.py::_hash_32 use FNV-1a32 (basis 0x811c9dc5, prime 0x01000193), expects 0x811c9dc5 for empty.
- C (lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:tuple_crc, lora_ping:packet_hash) uses crc32_ieee.
- Descriptions in README.md, schema.json, ccp*.json conflict on FNV vs keyed-CRC.
- ccp15_vectors() in generate.py (recently cleaned) uses unrelated 0x9e3779b9 multiplier for load_factor test.
Same named primitive produces different outputs across Python reference, Rust, Zephyr/C impls. Violates cross-impl consistency requirement for test vectors. See spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md and epic project-LICHEN-pgzj.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T01:59:01.853497662Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T02:04:33.357277211Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T02:04:33.357277211Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T02:09:33.357277211Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T02:04:33.357277211Z"}
